title: challenge-status output data
description:
Shape of the ``data`` field on a ``challenge-status`` envelope (C-verb).

The reduced per-challenge statuses (both views), per-citation read-time
disclosure, the ``contested`` counts block, and the fleet skip accounting â€”
plus the ``render`` markdown the agent relays VERBATIM and the ``view_sha``
(the canonical-JSON sha of the deterministic brief) a subsequent verdict may
carry so the gate can RECOMPUTE what-they-saw (the v1.6 rule). ``computed_at``
dates the whole projection.

computed_at.description: The instant the projection was computed against (ISO-8601 UTC).
computed_at.title: Computed At

challenges.description: The reduced challenges (thread: one; target view: all matching) â€” newest filed first.
challenges.title: Challenges

contested.description: The contested counts across the matched challenges (counts + identities).

skipped.description: Namespaces skipped during fleet collection (fail-open accounting).
skipped.title: Skipped

render.description: The deterministic markdown brief â€” relayed to the human verbatim.
render.title: Render

view_sha.description: Canonical-JSON sha of the rendered brief â€” the view a later verdict may bind and the gate recomputes.
view_sha.title: View Sha

ChallengeEntry.description:
One reduced challenge â€” its status, target, grounds, and (if ruled) verdict.

The lead of both views. ``status`` is the reduced per-challenge status;
``resolution`` is the target's read-time re-resolution (disclosed, never
refused); ``grounds`` is the challenger's opaque free-text dissent (echoed
verbatim, never parsed); ``verdict`` rides only when a ruling exists.

challenge_id.description: The challenge's caller-authored slug (opaque path segment).
challenge_id.title: Challenge Id

status.description: open | upheld | dismissed | withdrawn | superseded â€” the reduced status.
status.title: Status

filed_at.description: The filing record's ts â€” every challenge dated (the item ages while open).
filed_at.title: Filed At

target.description: The committed record this challenge attacks (address + resolver kind).

resolution.description: found-current | found-superseded | unresolvable â€” the target re-resolved at read.
resolution.title: Resolution

grounds.description: The challenger's free-text dissent â€” opaque, echoed verbatim, never interpreted.
grounds.title: Grounds

verdict.description: The human verdict record when the challenge was ruled; null while open/withdrawn.

ChallengeEntry.title: challenge entry

ChallengeTarget.description:
The committed record a challenge attacks â€” citation-shaped + the R3 address.

The ``kind`` dispatches target resolution through the SAME ``CITATION_KINDS``
resolver table evidence-memory owns (one definition). ``subject_kind`` /
``subject_id`` / ``content_sha`` are the full address the filing gate verified
exists as a committed record at exactly this sha â€” echoed here VERBATIM;
``subject_kind`` and ``subject_id`` are opaque caller identities, never parsed.

kind.description: The resolver-dispatch kind (closed set) â€” which store resolves the target.
kind.title: Kind

subject_kind.description: The challenged record's subject_kind (opaque caller identity; echoed, never parsed).
subject_kind.title: Subject Kind

subject_id.description: The challenged record's subject_id (opaque caller identity; echoed, never parsed).
subject_id.title: Subject Id

content_sha.description: The exact challenged content_sha (the full address's discriminator).
content_sha.title: Content Sha

ChallengeTarget.title: challenge target address

ChallengeVerdict.description:
The human verdict that resolved a challenge (C4) â€” present only when ruled.

``verdict`` is ``upheld`` | ``dismissed``; ``reasoning`` is the mandatory,
opaque, verbatim-echoed dissent-resolution prose (never parsed); ``ts`` dates
the ruling. Absent on an ``open`` or ``withdrawn`` challenge (the
emitted-only-when-present precedent). A verdict on a SINCE-superseded
challenge remains disclosed here even as ``status`` reads ``superseded``
(both reported; superseded wins the headline â€” C-reduce).

verdict.description: upheld | dismissed â€” the human's resolution of the challenge.
verdict.title: Verdict

reasoning.description: The human's free-text resolution rationale â€” opaque, echoed verbatim, never parsed.
reasoning.title: Reasoning

ts.description: The ruling's date (the verdict record's ts).
ts.title: Ts

ChallengeVerdict.title: challenge verdict record

CitationStatusLine.description:
Per-citation read-time re-resolution disclosure (the E read posture).

A challenge MUST cite evidence shas at filing (they resolve LIVE or the append
gate refuses). Evidence legitimately moves afterward (archived, re-exported,
wiped); the read side re-resolves each citation and reports ``verified`` per
line â€” the challenge stays a truthful dated record; the drift is disclosed,
the reader decides (the append gate is the deliberate refuse exception, never
run here). ``challenge_id`` scopes the line to its challenge.

challenge_id.description: The challenge this cited evidence belongs to (opaque slug).
challenge_id.title: Challenge Id

kind.description: The citation's resolver-dispatch kind (closed set) â€” its one resolver.
kind.title: Kind

ref.description: The opaque citation ref (run_id / cmd_sha key / dossier path); echoed, never parsed.
ref.title: Ref

sha.description: The full sha the challenge recorded for this citation.
sha.title: Sha

verified.description: True = re-resolved and the sha still matches here; False = unresolvable/mismatched at read.
verified.title: Verified

CitationStatusLine.title: challenge citation-status line

ContestedCounts.description:
The ``contested`` projection every disclosure seat carries (C-status).

Counts and identities, never a severity score (the attention-queue no-urgency
rule; a merit score is core grading dissent). Parallel to â€” never folded into
â€” any status vocabulary: a target can be ``current`` AND ``contested``. A
seat whose target has ALL-ZERO counts omits the block entirely (the
emitted-only-when-present precedent); this model is the shape when present.

superseded.description: Count of challenges the target's subject has moved past (computed).
superseded.title: Superseded

challenge_ids.description: The challenge slugs counted here (identities, never a ranking); opaque.
challenge_ids.title: Challenge Ids

ContestedCounts.title: challenge contested counts

SkippedNamespace.description:
One namespace skipped during fleet collection, with the reason (fail-open).

A wiped repo, an unreadable/torn ``repo.json``, or an absent store must never
crash the read â€” it is skipped silently and counted here (the evidence-memory
/ attention-queue ``SkippedNamespace`` posture, mirrored).

ref.description: The repo_hash / namespace id that was skipped.
ref.title: Ref

reason.description: Why it was skipped (unreadable, torn, absent).
reason.title: Reason

SkippedNamespace.title: challenge skipped namespace
